claim: BTC is still more likely than not to resolve Yes, but the best post-synthesis view is below the market: the contract is a narrow single-minute Binance close with only a modest current cushion above 72k, so mid-80s confidence looks somewhat rich absent stronger independent volatility-based verification.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: final settlement references Binance website candle rather than directly verified website capture in synthesis pass
independently_verified_points: Contract resolves on Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle Close on Apr 17
Current Binance BTCUSDT remains around 74063 on Apr 15, modestly above 72000
Recent Binance 1-minute prices are clustered near 74k rather than barely above strike
No scheduled FOMC decision falls before the Apr 17 noon ET cutoff
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No strong independent volatility or options-based check was added to quantify the chance of a 2-3% drawdown into the exact minute.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: Current Binance spot is already about 2.8% above strike with no major scheduled macro catalyst before cutoff, so market mid-80s pricing may simply be fair.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Personas differ mainly on how much probability discount the exact-minute settlement mechanic deserves versus current above-strike spot.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes requires the Binance BTC/USDT Apr 17 12:00 ET 1-minute candle final Close to be strictly above 72000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: short-horizon BTC volatility into the exact Apr 17 noon ET Binance settlement minute
decision_blockers: Residual uncertainty about ordinary BTC volatility over the remaining ~48 hours
No independent quantified volatility check beyond lane-level spot/context work
Final answer remains highly sensitive to price location on Apr 16-17 rather than today's snapshot
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance BTC/USDT and nearby threshold pricing late Apr 16 or early Apr 17 ET.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 84.5% Yes. My syndicated range is 76% to 82% Yes. That points to a modest below-market view rather than an actionable large edge. The likely mispricing is that traders may be overweighting spot being above 72k and underweighting that this resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. No personas were missing. I critically checked raw findings against sidecars; the sidecars looked broadly faithful, with catalyst-hunter the most aggressive on probability but still fairly represented. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were not needed beyond the raw findings plus bounded synthesis-stage verification. Coverage is complete because all intended lanes were present and coherent.
sections.Market-implied baseline: Baseline is 0.845 Yes at the provided market snapshot. The swarm median was 0.76, already below market. Nothing in the synthesis-stage checks justified moving above market; the main question was whether to stay near the swarm center or compress upward toward market.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: Final post-synthesis estimate: 0.76 to 0.82 Yes. That keeps Yes as the base case because Binance BTCUSDT is currently around 74,063, but holds below market because a roughly 2.8% cushion is not especially large for BTC over two days when the contract cares about one exact minute.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This range is only modestly above the provisional swarm center of 0.76. I moved slightly upward because fresh synthesis verification confirmed Binance spot remains near 74k and reinforced the no-near-term-FOMC point. I did not move all the way toward market because the additional verification still did not independently show that a mid-80s probability is warranted for a single-minute crypto threshold contract.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I disagree moderately with the market. Directionally the market is right that Yes is favored, but I do not think the evidence supports 84.5% with much confidence. The disagreement is mostly about confidence calibration, not direction.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Independent verification was medium quality. I rechecked live Binance BTCUSDT via direct API, confirming current price around 74,063 and recent 1-minute closes clustered near 74k. I also checked the Federal Reserve calendar and confirmed the next FOMC meeting is Apr 28-29, so there is no scheduled FOMC decision before resolution. Those checks validate the basic bullish setup and remove one obvious scheduled macro catalyst, but they do not fully verify the below-market edge because they do not quantify the probability of an ordinary 2-3% downside move into the exact settlement minute.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. I did not materially compress toward market because the fresh checks supported the existing swarm view more than the market. I did make only a small upward adjustment from the swarm center rather than staying at the lowest lane estimates, because current Binance context remains clearly above strike. But the synthesis did not find enough independent support to adopt the market's mid-80s confidence.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is late Apr 16 into Apr 17 morning ET. The edge is likely to compress toward market if BTC holds comfortably above roughly 73.5k-74k into that window, and widen against market if BTC drifts back toward 72k. Waiting for a closer spot check is more likely to improve accuracy because this market is extremely freshness-sensitive.
sections.Decision blockers: There is no major contract blocker. The real blockers are timing sensitivity, lack of a quantified volatility cross-check, and the fact that today's edge can decay quickly if BTC remains firm into settlement. This is enough to justify caution but not enough to force new research before forming a view.
sections.Implication for the question: As of the synthesis pass, Yes remains more likely than No, but not enough to justify strong conviction above the current market-implied level. Operationally: lean Yes, but treat any perceived No edge as confidence-calibration rather than a strong directional inversion.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed the contract mechanics are clear and exact-minute sensitive. All agreed BTC was currently above 72k on Binance by a modest but meaningful margin. All agreed Yes is favored. Four of five lanes thought the market was at least somewhat too confident because of one-minute settlement fragility; the market-implied lane was closest to market and still slightly below it.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: Main disagreement: timing/weighting. Catalyst-hunter treated the current above-strike cushion plus quiet scheduled calendar as enough for 88%, while base-rate, risk-manager, and variant-view discounted more heavily for exact-minute settlement and ordinary BTC volatility. Secondary disagreement: market-pricing interpretation. Market-implied thought the nearby threshold ladder looked broadly coherent, while the more skeptical lanes thought the market was compressing a narrow settlement condition into a simpler spot-regime story.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest surviving countercase is catalyst-hunter plus parts of market-implied: BTC is already trading materially above strike on the governing venue, no major scheduled FOMC event sits before cutoff, and only a modest hold-the-line outcome is needed, so mid-80s pricing may be fair.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance API context is directionally representative of settlement; no exchange anomaly distorts the final candle; no major new negative shock arrives before Apr 17 noon ET. Contested assumptions: whether a ~2.7-2.9% cushion is large enough to justify mid-80s odds; whether current market pricing already fully incorporates exact-minute settlement fragility. Fragile assumptions: short-horizon realized volatility remains ordinary rather than shock-like; Binance-specific microstructure does not produce an adverse noon print.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: current Binance BTCUSDT near 74k; recent minute closes clustered near 74k; contract wording explicit on venue/pair/time/close field; no scheduled FOMC decision before cutoff. Strongest contradictory evidence: BTC can move 2-3% in two days without extraordinary news; only the exact noon ET Binance minute matters; settlement depends on one venue and one candle close. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket contract wording and Binance venue-specific price context. Ambiguous evidence: no direct independent volatility model or options-implied move estimate was added in synthesis.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to explicit contract mechanics and same-venue Binance price context. Moderate weight went to the lack of scheduled FOMC catalyst because it reduces one obvious event risk but does not eliminate unscheduled shocks. I downweighted generalized BTC bullishness and any argument that current spot alone should map directly to settlement probability. I also downweighted the catalyst-hunter's 88% as somewhat aggressive relative to the same fragility facts acknowledged elsewhere.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest evidence against the syndicated below-market view is simply that BTC is already above strike by around 2.8% on the settling venue, with only about two days left and no known major scheduled macro event before cutoff. If spot remains in the mid-74k area into Apr 17 morning, the market's mid-80s may prove fair or even conservative.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: Contract ambiguity is minor, not material. The operative interpretation is straightforward: use Binance, use BTC/USDT, use the Apr 17 12:00 ET 1-minute candle, and use the final Close; it must be strictly greater than 72,000. The only small residual source-of-truth caveat is that synthesis verification relied on Binance API/context rather than capturing the exact website candle interface cited in the rules.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Any alpha here comes from confidence calibration. If the market is treating an exact-minute threshold contract too much like a broad 'BTC regime above 72k' question, Yes may be overpriced by several points. But this can also destroy alpha quickly if BTC simply holds firm, because the time-to-resolution is short and the market can converge sharply toward realized spot conditions.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A fresh Binance check closer to settlement showing BTC still comfortably above 74.5k with stable intraday action would push the estimate upward toward market or above it. Conversely, BTC slipping back toward 72k, rising intraday downside volatility, or any Binance-specific dislocation would move the estimate materially lower. The most important falsifier is realized price location near the strike on Apr 16-17.
sections.Highest-value next research: One final same-venue check of Binance BTC/USDT on Apr 16 evening or Apr 17 morning ET, ideally paired with a quick nearby-threshold ladder snapshot or short-horizon implied-move estimate.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class: governing contract language plus same-venue Binance market data. Most important secondary source class: official Fed calendar for scheduled macro timing. Evidence independence is medium at best because the analysis remains heavily anchored to the same venue that settles the contract. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low overall. The synthesis is not bottlenecked by missing personas, but it is somewhat bottlenecked by the lack of a stronger independent volatility quantification layer.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, synthesis-stage verification was used. It confirmed the swarm's basic setup rather than overturning it: Binance spot remains above strike and there is no scheduled FOMC decision before resolution. Cross-lane comparison also showed that most disagreement is not factual but probabilistic weighting of the exact-minute mechanic. The synthesis judged catalyst-hunter directionally plausible but a bit overconfident versus the common fragility evidence.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — strongest outside-view reminder that single-timestamp crypto markets deserve a discount versus broad regime intuition. catalyst-hunter — best articulation of the bullish hold-the-line case and explicit scheduled-catalyst check. market-implied — best defense of treating market pricing as a serious prior and of using the nearby ladder as coherence evidence. risk-manager — clearest framing of confidence-calibration risk and why a ~2.7% cushion is not large for BTC. variant-view — strongest concise minority framing that the real issue is market overconfidence, not a directional No thesis.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: exact-minute crypto threshold markets should not be mapped too mechanically from current spot distance to strike. Possible missing driver: short-horizon realized/implied volatility calibration is still underbuilt relative to how important it is in these contracts. Possible source-quality lesson: same-venue checks are necessary but not sufficient when the market edge depends on probability-of-drawdown rather than current level. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: the main recurring gap is not contract reading but lightweight volatility calibration for narrow crypto threshold markets.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for a closer checkpoint rather than forcing more broad research now. Re-run a tight verification pass on Binance BTC/USDT and, if available, a quick implied-move or realized-vol sanity check late Apr 16 or early Apr 17 ET before any final downstream decision.
